diagrams.title: Training vs Validation Loss Curve
diagrams.description: A line graph showing training loss steadily decreasing while validation loss initially decreases then starts increasing after a certain point. This visualizes the classic overfitting phenomenon where the model learns training data too well but loses generalization ability.
elements: Training loss curve (decreasing, going toward zero)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how excessive gradient descent iterations lead to overfitting, showing the divergence between training and validation performance

diagrams.title: Gradient Descent on Complex Loss Landscape
diagrams.description: A 3D-like loss surface with contour lines showing a wide global minimum (good generalization) and narrow local minima (overfitting). Arrows show gradient descent paths converging to different minima based on initialization and learning rate.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how gradient descent can converge to sharp, narrow minima that overfit training data versus broader minima that generalize better

diagrams.title: Early Stopping Visualization
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing a model at optimal iteration (good fit) vs model after excessive iterations (overfit). Includes decision boundaries or fitted curves comparing both states.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the solution of early stopping - stopping gradient descent before overfitting occurs - as a key regularization technique
